items.hook: The Knesset passes the controversial Nationality Bill, which defines the State of Israel as the nation-state of the Jewish people.
items.body: The Knesset is the unicameral legislature of Israel. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: ‘Colony’ director Yeon Sang-ho talks sequel plans through novel, video game
items.body: EXCLUSIVE: The film has proved a box office hit in South Korea and is set for a US release on August 28.

items.hook: Spanish Civil War: The CNT and UGT call a general strike in Spain – mobilizing workers' militias against the Nationalist forces. People's Olympiad of Barcelona cancelled.
items.body: The Spanish Civil War was fought from 1936 to 1939 between the Republicans and the Nationalist rebels. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Huntley Meadows Park, the largest park operated by the Fairfax County Park Authority, is located in the Hybla Valley area of Fairfax County, Virginia, south of the city of Alexandria.
items.body: The park features a visitor center, a beaver-created wetland with boardwalk, wildlife observation platforms, and an interpretative trail system. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Matt Wingard is an American politician in the state of Oregon.
items.body: He was appointed to fill a vacancy in the Oregon House of Representatives in 2008, and was elected to the seat in 2008 and re-elected in 2010. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner for My Left Foot, dies aged 81
items.body:
The acclaimed Irish actor started her career in Coronation Street and Casualty before a string of high-profile Hollywood roles

Brenda Fricker, who became the first female Irish Oscar winner for acting with My Left Foot, has died ag

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".

items.hook: Italian War of 1542–46: The first Siege of Boulogne begins.
items.body: The Italian War of 1542–1546 was a conflict late in the Italian Wars, pitting Francis I of France and Suleiman I of the Ottoman Empire against the Holy Roman Emperor Charles V and Henry VIII of England. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Luise Kraushaar was a German political activist who became a Resistance campaigner against National Socialism and who also, after she left Germany, worked in the French Resistance.
items.body: She later became a historian, employed at Berlin's Marxism–Leninism Institute in the German Democratic Republic. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Established in 1870, it is the oldest public library in Kerala.
items.body: Ernakulam Public Library is a public library in Kochi in the state of Kerala, India. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Memorial City is a commercial district in the Memorial area of Houston, Texas, United States.
items.body: Located along Interstate 10 between Beltway 8 and Bunker Hill Road, the district is anchored by Memorial City Mall, the nation's 38th-largest shopping mall; Memorial Hermann Memorial City Medical Center; CityCentre, a high-density mixed-use development; and To

items.hook: The Great Fire of Rome causes widespread devastation and rages on for six days, destroying half of the city.
items.body: AD 64 (LXIV) was a leap year starting on Sunday of the Julian calendar, the 64th Year of the Anno Domini designation, the 64th year of the 1st millennium, the 64th year of the 1st century, and the 4th year of the 7th decade. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: Bruno Mars has an unexpected opening before Wembley party starts
items.body: After an eight-year absence from UK stages, Bruno Mars returned to Wembley Stadium with a polished, hit-filled show.

items.hook: A car bomb kills Judge Paolo Borsellino and five members of his escort.
items.body: The via D'Amelio bombing was a terrorist attack by the Sicilian Mafia, which took place in Palermo, Sicily, Italy, on 19 July 1992. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: World War I: Battle of Fromelles: British and Australian troops attack German trenches as part of the Battle of the Somme.
items.body: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The 34th Don Carlos Palanca Memorial Awards for Literature was held to commemorate the memory of Don Carlos Palanca Sr.
items.body: through an endeavor that would promote education and culture in the country. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The 2018 NCAA Division I men's soccer tournament was the 60th edition of the NCAA Division I men's soccer tournament, a postseason tournament to determine the national champion of NCAA Division I men's college soccer.
items.body: The first four rounds of the competition were held at the home ground of the higher seed, while the College Cup were held at Harder Stadium on the campus of University of California, Santa Barbara. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.
